content: Hey, I'm back from my sabbatical and I'm still trying to get used to being back in the States. I spent the last 6 months traveling through Eastern Europe and it was amazing.
Welcome back! That sounds like an incredible experience. What was your favorite part of the trip?
I think it was the people I met along the way. I stayed with a family in Budapest for a week and they taught me all about beekeeping, which I've always been interested in.
That's fascinating! I've never met anyone who's into beekeeping. Are you planning on starting your own hive now that you're back?
Yeah, I'd love to. I've already started looking into local beekeeping clubs in New York City, where I live. I'm hoping to join one and learn more about it.
That's great! It's always good to have a hobby that gets you outdoors. So, what do you do for work, if you don't mind me asking?
I'm a civil rights attorney, so I'm looking forward to getting back to work and fighting for some good causes. I've been out of the loop for a while, so I'm a bit nervous about getting back into it.
That's really admirable. I'm sure you'll get back into the swing of things quickly. Have you had a chance to catch up with your family and friends since you've been back?
Yeah, I've seen my family a few times. My mom is actually coming to visit me next weekend, on the 17th of March. I'm looking forward to spending some quality time with her.
That sounds like fun! What are you planning on doing with your mom while she's in town?
We're going to check out some comedy clubs, I've recently gotten into stand-up comedy and I think she'll enjoy it too. Maybe we'll even catch a show on the 18th.
Stand-up comedy is a great way to spend time together. I've heard that rock climbing is also a fun activity to do with family, have you tried that before?
Actually, yes! I've been rock climbing for years, and I love it. Maybe I'll take my mom to the climbing gym near my apartment in Brooklyn.
That sounds like a great idea. I'm sure your mom will appreciate the effort you put into planning activities for her visit.
Yeah, I hope so. I just want to make sure she feels welcome and has a good time. I've been thinking about planning a trip for us to go to the Adirondacks together, maybe in May or June.
The Adirondacks are beautiful, I'm sure you and your mom will have a great time. Let me know if you need any help planning the trip.
Thanks, I'll definitely consider that. I think that's all for now, it was nice chatting with you.
You too, it was great talking to you. Have a great day and enjoy your time with your mom!
You too, thanks again.
No problem, take care.
